category_title: Nonverbal students with ASD using iPads for communication and academic tasks
minimal_context: The student is using the Speech with Milo app on an iPad in the classroom to learn prepositions.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for the Speech with Milo app. Touch Milo the mouse.

STUDENT: (Touches Milo the mouse on the iPad.)

TUTOR: Listen to the preposition.

STUDENT: (Advances the screen before the audio description is completed.)

TUTOR: Let's go back. Touch Milo and watch what he does.

STUDENT: (Touches Milo the mouse, but advances the screen before Milo demonstrates the preposition.)

options.A: Allow the student to withdraw from the classroom for a time-limited period to prevent emotional overload.
options.B: Remain directly present with the student while they interact with the Speech with Milo app.
options.C: Strategically back away from the student, quietly stepping into the background to camouflage the tutor's presence.
options.D: Respond to the screen advance by saying, 'Another one already? No way! Good job, keep going.'
